facts: Our Sun fits 1.3 MILLION Earths inside it!
But meet Stephenson 2-18, the LARGEST known star.
If Stephenson 2-18 replaced our Sun, its edge would extend PAST Saturn's entire orbit.
Saturn, orbiting 1.4 billion km (870 million miles) from our Sun, would be *inside* Stephenson 2-18!
idea.payoff: Our entire inner solar system, swallowed whole! 🤯
idea.title: Star that EATS Planets! 🤯🪐

scenes.visual: A bright, prominent Sun filling much of the screen, with a tiny, visibly orbiting Earth.
The Sun remains. Small Earth icons rapidly appear and 'fill' the Sun's outline, illustrating its volume capacity.

scenes.text: But meet Stephenson 2-18, the LARGEST known star.
scenes.visual: The Sun rapidly shrinks and slides off-screen to the left. A colossal, deep red supergiant star (Stephenson 2-18) slowly slides in from the right, appearing to dwarf the previous Sun's size.

scenes.text: Its edge would extend PAST Saturn's orbit!
scenes.visual: Stephenson 2-18 now dominates the screen. A faint, glowing outline of Saturn's orbit (with Saturn's tiny icon) appears, completely engulfed and well within the star's fiery boundary.

scenes.text: Our entire inner solar system, SWALLOWED WHOLE! 🤯
scenes.visual: A dramatic, full-screen view of Stephenson 2-18. Faint, ghosted concentric circles representing the orbits of Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, and Saturn are visible, all completely submerged within the star's immense scale. The star subtly pulsates with light.
